summary: Saudi Aramco is in talks with Ukrainian drone manufacturers SkyFall and Wild Hornets to acquire interceptor drones for oil field protection, while also engaging Phantom Defense for electronic warfare systems. On the battlefield, Russian forces are deploying FPV drones in coordinated formations of 3-5 units, with operators using systems like "Yolka" to kinetically intercept Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs. The "Rubikon" center reported 111 targets destroyed on March 12 and 104 on March 11, focusing on personnel strikes and FPV air defense. Ukrainian forces are adapting by using FPV drones to target Russian infantry, including a reported incident where a Russian soldier charged a drone with a bayonet and was destroyed. Ukraine's procurement of multirotors like Mavic, Autel, and Matrice has doubled compared to last year, with a 17% increase in February deliveries.
